what_it_does: Records the outcome and by-use verification narrative of the G24 build lane, which fixed a schema-passthrough defect in the fabric transport (fabric/client.py, transport.py) so that role output schemas reach the vLLM guided-decode path. Lists files written, status, needs_tim, and a detailed root-cause/notes block.
observations: files_written: fabric/client.py, fabric/transport.py, fabric/AGENTS.md, and the report itself.

Root cause traced: run_role passed schema=role.output_schema into complete() as a NAMED param, so it never reached the transport's **opts; _apply_response_format only saw json=True and produced unconstrained json_object decode.
Reproduced first: 3 known-failing files (bridge.py, roles/check.py, nodes/portal.py) deterministically failed with FabricError unparseable JSON, each burning ~15s of retries.

Fix: complete() now derives opts['json_schema'] from its schema= param (name + model_json_schema()) unless json_schema is explicitly passed; characterizes the change as finishing the already-built L-transport seam rather than new code.
Re-fired regression set: all three files return schema-conformant digests in 0.5-0.7s with no retries.
Lists ~15 test suites reported green (json_schema_transport 17, cascade_acceptance 26, coa_acceptance 27, mcp_engine_acceptance 35, etc.).
Self-classifies status as built-verified; states it was run against a resident 4B (cyankiwi/Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ) with GPU floor confirmed before and after.

needs_tim notes a non-blocking flag: the coa framing call now also rides guided decode, which degrades via existing loud-degrade path if the brain is rebound to a cloud endpoint that 400s on json_schema.
standouts: The traced root cause: a named param silently not reaching **opts, reproduced deterministically before fixing.
Frames the fix as completing a half-built seam (L-transport) with zero new transport code paths.
Flags a forward risk (coa on a cloud model that rejects json_schema) without it gating the lane.
conventions: Follows the seam-lane report schema: lane, files_written, how_verified, status, needs_tim, notes.
Heavy reproduce-then-fix-then-re-verify narrative with cited file:line and suite counts, matching the repo's evidence discipline.
Notes files not committed per lane instruction and distinguishes its own files from other lanes' in-flight files.
notes: A build-lane status record: the G24 lane's self-report that the fabric schema-passthrough fix landed and was verified by re-firing the previously failing ingest set.
questions: Was fabric/AGENTS.md's update reflected in the wider self-description registry, or only locally?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it is a build report and it records a build outcome with verification evidence.
intention_signals: Aims to prove a transport-layer fix was the missing hand-off (schema never reaching opts), verified end-to-end against a live model, while flagging a downstream cloud-model degrade risk so it is not a surprise.
novelty: The specific named-param-never-reaches-opts root cause and the framing of the fix as completing a previously proven-but-unwired transport branch.
contribution: Contributes a documented, evidence-traced account of the fabric schema-passthrough fix and the guided-decode wiring decision, plus the coa-on-cloud-400 degrade flag, that a fused system would keep as the provenance for that transport behavior.
summary_for_embedding: A seam-lane JSON report documenting the G24 fix that routes role output schemas through the fabric transport into vLLM guided decoding, with reproduce/fix/re-verify evidence.
intention_for_embedding: To record and justify completing the L-transport schema-passthrough seam so role schemas constrain decoding, verified by-use against the resident 4B.
